data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
There are a lot of exciting breakthroughs happening in clean energy. A few decades from now, I expect that at least one of these technologies will be generating a significant chunk of the world’s power. 

Here are the innovations I’m tracking: https://lnkd.in/g5TiH9pN

original_post.text:
In the midst of a changing climate, knowing how to adapt and prepare can make all the difference.

For smallholder farmers, timely information helps them act quickly to protect their crops, livestock, and livelihoods. That’s why the Gates Foundation supports partners like Digital Green that provide real-time weather alerts right when farmers need them, helping them make informed decisions and increase their crop yields.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Real-time weather alerts are helping farmers protect their crops and livelihoods. Such a simple idea with a major impact.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Finding new pathways to reliable energy is critical. Fortunately, major breakthroughs in electricity are paving the way for a more abundant—and affordable—energy future.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
In remote and hard-to-reach communities, many children grow up without receiving vaccines—simply because health services can't reach them. In Uganda, this is changing. Thanks to investments in transport, cold chain systems, and support for local health workers, the number of unimmunized children has been cut nearly in half.

While progress is underway, thousands of children remain unprotected. But with every new tool and investment, the chance for every child to receive lifesaving vaccines is increasing.

original_post.text:
Where is energy innovation headed next? BE leaders reflect on what they’re seeing from the front lines:
 
▪️ Bill Gates on the technologies shaping the next era of electricity.
▪️ Rodi Guidero on what founders, investors, and companies across the portfolio shared with us during the BEV Investor Summit.
▪️ Eric Toone on why shifting cost curves are bringing us closer to a clean energy system.
▪️ Carmichael Roberts on the human side of company building in tough industries.
 
Signals from the builders, investors, and systems transforming the energy economy.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
Access to affordable, reliable energy is fundamental to prosperity—and to improving lives around the world. ​
 ​
We’re seeing real progress from the people building the next generation of energy solutions. Their insights help show what it will take to expand opportunities, strengthen economies, and ensure more people can benefit from a clean energy future.

original_post.poster_title: President of Africa & India Offices and Chief Strategy Officer at Gates Foundation
original_post.text:
I just returned from Nairobi—my first trip to Africa as President of the Gates Foundation's Africa and India Office (AIO). I felt an overwhelming sense of optimism as I watched long-term leaders in the field stand up alongside a new generation of innovators to face challenging headwinds.

We convened at a critical moment. The ground is shifting quickly. Across much of Africa, funding is tightening, debt is rising, and the model that many of us have worked in for decades is under strain.

The stakes are high, with child mortality increasing for the first time this century last year.

And yet, what I’ve seen this week gives me incredible hope. Locally-led innovations, from ag-tech to digital finance tools, are already improving the lives of people across the continent.

What’s needed now is getting these innovations to more people and places, a point that came up in nearly every conversation.

That means working closely with local leaders and investing in solutions that countries can nurture and sustain on their own. I saw many examples of that during my visit, including at InsectiPro, where I watched flies turn organic waste into animal feed and fertilizer—innovation that is locally-owned, managed, and directly improving the lives of the community.

It also means countries across the Global South sharing what works with one another. We created AIO to do just that—both bringing decision-making closer to the communities we serve and to strengthen South-South collaboration from within. And leaving Nairobi, I feel hope for our ability to support these aims.

I’m also leaving with an enduring and refreshed gratitude to the teams and partners in Kenya, and across Gates Foundation Africa, who are doing this work every day. Your commitment to genuine, honest, and tireless collaboration is what makes progress possible.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: So much to be hopeful about. The solutions are already there; the work now is supporting communities to scale them.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Timely prevention is one of the most effective tools we have in the fight against malaria—and volunteers like Abednego Afreh are spreading the word to their communities. By strapping a megaphone to his tricycle, Afreh broadcasts health education messages across Kajaji, Ghana to make sure every household is ready to receive preventive treatment when volunteers arrive. This malaria season, his dedication helped 5,302 children get protected.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: As a child, Dr. Kelly Chibale survived a life-threatening malaria infection thanks to timely treatment and access to essential medicines. Years later, he and his team of African-based scientists discovered a promising new malaria drug that advanced to clinical trials—the first time any disease treatment had reached this stage through an African-led effort. Stories like his make me hopeful about the future of global health progress.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: One of the most encouraging things about climate innovation is how many zero-carbon solutions are becoming cost-competitive—and how they’re improving people’s lives in the process. We’re seeing real breakthroughs that make clean technologies easier to scale and adopt, which means progress can be made much faster and benefit more people along the way.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: I'm enormously optimistic about Africa's future and encouraged by the ambition of its leaders. The Economist recently made the case for the continent’s resilience in purely economic terms. I’d argue that true strength is measured by how many children are surviving and thriving. And after last year's aid cuts, child mortality went up for the first time this century. I wrote a letter to the editor making the case for a more expansive definition of prosperity that puts people at the center.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Coaches at Grassroot Soccer do much more than run drills. Through mentorship and access to HIV healthcare, they're helping teenagers live their healthiest lives and bringing us one step closer to an AIDS-free generation.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Breakthroughs in fusion have the potential to help us produce the clean energy we need by 2050. One of the great things about this energy source is that once it’s fully commercialized, it can be built anywhere and scaled up—with no carbon emissions and minimal waste. Here are a few other promising technologies that could help us get to a zero-emission future: https://lnkd.in/gbw9VMTc

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
This is huge news for the fight against tuberculosis. The World Health Organization is recommending near point-of-care tests that can use tongue swabs to collect samples and diagnose TB within an hour—making testing faster, simpler, and more accessible.​
​
Tools like this will reach more people, catch TB earlier, and start treatment sooner. Grateful to the scientists and partners who made this progress possible. https://b-gat.es/4clCCIx

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: The breakthroughs happening in geothermal, fusion, and geologic hydrogen have the potential to help make clean electricity widely available to more people.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
Reflecting on my time at the Breakthrough Energy Ventures Investor Summit last week, I’m left incredibly energized.

The pace of progress is exceeding even my most optimistic expectations. It’s clear that real breakthroughs are happening, and they’re happening faster and more effectively than I imagined.

What’s most exciting is the potential impact: These advances will help make reliable energy more accessible to more people around the world. That kind of momentum is inspiring to be part of.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Well-lit rooms, reliable power, and cold storage for medicine are things many of us don’t think twice about—but in rural health facilities across Uganda, they’re far from guaranteed. Programs like this, which ensure access to reliable energy, can make a huge difference in people’s lives. https://b-gat.es/3PzhGFj

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
There’s something powerful about a room full of people committed to building, deploying, and scaling the next generation of clean energy.

At the Breakthrough Energy Ventures Investor Summit, I was reminded just how foundational affordable, reliable energy is to opportunity and prosperity.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
I expect that in a few decades, at least one of these technologies will be generating a significant share of the world’s power: ​
​
Geothermal - New innovations are making it more accessible than ever before, with companies now drilling deeper and extending wells horizontally to unlock heat in more places.
 ​
Fusion - If successfully commercialized, it could be built almost anywhere, scaled up, and used to generate massive amounts of electricity. ​

Geologic hydrogen - One of the biggest energy surprises of the past decade. It's still in its earliest stage, but the potential is exciting.​
​
The progress is real—and it’s opening up new paths to a clean energy future.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: When Breakthrough Energy launched more than a decade ago, energy innovation was scarce, even as demand for cleaner and more affordable options was growing. Today, breakthroughs are emerging across the entire system—and more people will thrive around the world as a result.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: I’m excited to be at the Breakthrough Energy Ventures Investors Summit this week. It’s one of the best places to see the future of energy, and I can’t wait to see how much progress has been made since the last time we came together: https://lnkd.in/gT66_N_b

original_post.text:
We are thrilled to announce Biomni-AD and Prima Mente as the winners of the Alzheimer's Insights AI Prize.

We are doubling the grand prize, and both teams will be taking home $1M each.

This competition was originally designed to award a single $1M prize, but the insights that have emerged from this competition have far exceeded our expectations and we’re excited to double the prize pot to award two grand prizes. The expansion of the prize reflects the exceptional quality of submissions and the pressing need for innovative approaches in the field.

Congratulations to the teams:

Biomni-AD: Kuan-lin Huang Kexin Huang Stanford University Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai

Prima Mente: Ravi Solanki Jonathan Hoover Yash Shirsath

To all our finalist teams – we have been awed by your hard work, creativity, and dedication to advancing Alzheimer's research. This community continues to push the boundaries of what is possible, and we’re looking forward to seeing what comes next.

A special thank you to our expert judging panel for the thoughtful evaluation and scientific rigor in both the finalist and semifinalist rounds: Gregory Moore MD, PhD Justine Levin-Allerhand Aashima Gupta Zoe Kourtzi Elena Bonfiglioli Tom Stocky Eric Horvitz Hoifung Poon

The work doesn't stop here. Stay tuned to learn when Biomni-AD and Prima Mente’s tools become available on AD Workbench.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
Over the next 25 years, around 152 million people are projected to be living with Alzheimer's. The good news is that new tools are helping us better understand the disease and move faster towards potential treatments. 
 
That's why we created the Alzheimer's Insights AI Prize. Out of 180+ submissions, two teams stood out to the judges. One is building an AI co-scientist that can take lots of messy data (genes, proteins, experiments), make sense of it, and run reproducible research workflows. The other is developing a multi-agent platform that uses virtual neurons and brain cells to model disease and test therapies digitally. 
 
I've long believed AI will be a powerful tool to dramatically accelerate medical breakthroughs—and these two teams are a good example of that potential. Congratulations to both on this win.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: By 2050, the world will need nearly three times as much energy as we use today. With breakthroughs in geothermal, nuclear, and hydrogen energy, I believe it’s possible to get there in a clean, reliable, and affordable way:  https://lnkd.in/gkBddWwb

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
Breakthrough Energy backs practical, scalable solutions that strengthen our energy supply while cutting emissions. Redwood Materials is an example of that approach in action. ​
​
As AI drives rapid construction of data centers—and increases demand for reliable, around-the-clock electricity—Redwood Materials has stepped up to expand its energy storage business using old EV batteries, quadrupling its San Francisco footprint to help meet that need.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
New tools are helping countries better anticipate malaria risk and respond faster. ​
​
That’s increasingly important as climate change and extreme weather shift where and when malaria spreads. Floods, in particular, create the perfect conditions for outbreaks—because the standing water they leave behind becomes a breeding ground for mosquitoes. ​
​
Projects like the Remote Sensing for Malaria Control in Africa program at the University of Pretoria are helping change that. By combining satellite imagery with environmental modeling, health teams can now predict where mosquito populations are most likely to soar and get prevention tools to those areas while disaster response is still underway.  ​
 ​
It's a great example of how integrating disease prevention into disaster planning can make both systems work better. Continued innovation and support for tools like these will be critical to protecting the progress we’ve made against malaria.

original_post.text:
Ten years after Breakthrough Energy was founded to build companies in some of the world’s most consequential markets, the real story belongs to the founders, engineers, and operators doing the hard work of turning breakthrough science into operating infrastructure. 
 
Our Executive Director Rodi Guidero shares an update to investors highlighting teams leading that effort: 
  
▪️Fervo Energy is advancing commercial-scale geothermal power. 
▪️Commonwealth Fusion Systems is building fusion infrastructure. 
▪️Form Energy is manufacturing long-duration storage. 
▪️LuxWall, Inc. and Aeroseal are driving down energy use and cost in buildings.  
▪️KoBold Metals is modernizing mineral discovery. 
▪️Pivot Bio and Rumin8 are reshaping agricultural productivity. 
 
These companies are navigating permitting, construction, supply chains, and customer adoption—the practical realities of building in essential industries. Their progress points toward a future where decarbonization is driven by better, cost-competitive products operating at scale.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: What excites me the most about clean energy right now is the opportunity to close the gap between innovation and infrastructure—and shape the future of human prosperity in the process.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: For nearly 40 years, pharmaceutical drug donations have played a transformative role in combating neglected tropical diseases. These programs deliver medicine at scale, making it possible for countries’ public health systems to prevent and treat NTDs at no cost to patients. The collaboration and generosity that enable these donations are critical to eliminating NTDs for good—and one of global health’s greatest success stories.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Saidi Jafari lost his wife and daughter to childbirth complications. Today, he’s an m-mama driver—doing his part to make sure that no other family has to suffer that same fate.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
Geothermal has the potential to be one of our most reliable and scalable sources of clean energy, and the expertise to unlock it already exists.    

By applying the experience of oil and gas engineers, drillers, and scientists, companies like Fervo Energy are shaping the next generation of clean energy and demonstrating how an industry fueled by innovation can deliver dependable power while creating high-quality jobs for a skilled workforce.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
The best hospital in the world could be just ten miles away. But if a pregnant woman in crisis can’t reach it, it might as well not exist for her. ​​
​​
That’s why m-mama created a kind of 911 for rural Africa—coordinating cars, boats, motorbikes, and community drivers to get women in labor to the care they need when every minute counts.

original_post.text:
A decisive step forward in stopping polio outbreaks — at speed and at scale.

Biological E. Limited has won Phase II Pre-Qualification from the World Health Organization for nOPV2, validating fully integrated manufacturing from drug substance to finished vaccine — all at one site.

700M+ doses already powering global response. Now with stronger, end-to-end WHO-qualified capacity when the world needs it most.

Link to the full article is in the comments.

#nOPV2 #Polio #PolioEradication #WHO #PreQualification #BiologicalE #BE

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: India’s Biological E achieved a major milestone and is now manufacturing a lifesaving polio vaccine that will be critical to stopping certain outbreaks of the virus. It’s just one more example of why, despite the setbacks we saw last year, global health progress is a bell that can’t be unrung.

original_post.poster_title: Executive Director, Breakthrough Energy & Managing Partner, Breakthrough Energy Ventures
original_post.text:
In an update to investors, I reflect on a decade of building in the world’s foundational industries with Breakthrough Energy—and where I see the next phase of industrial innovation heading. 
 
For several years, we’ve been focused on a core question: what does it actually take to build enduring businesses in markets measured in trillions, with infrastructure designed to last for generations? 
 
It takes technical depth, patient capital, relentless attention to cost, and the discipline to back companies that can become household names in essential industries. 
 
I share how our strategy has evolved, what we’re seeing across the portfolio, and why this moment matters for long-term investors.  
 
I look forward to continuing the conversation at our BEV Investor Summit and at CERAWeek later this month.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: A decade ago, transforming how the world was built and powered felt unlikely. Today, it's happening. Breakthrough Energy is focused on helping make clean solutions the most practical and affordable options. Rodi shares more about how we’re doing this in his letter to investors:

original_post.text:
🚀 Huge news for people living with sleeping sickness!

Acoziborole, a one-day, single-dose oral treatment, has received a positive opinion from the European Medicines Agency (EMA) for the treatment of the most common form of sleeping sickness, T.b. gambiense.

💊 DNDi’s Dr Wilfried remembers treating patients with the old medicine which was so toxic it killed one in twenty patients. Developed together with Sanofi, acoziborole is a revolution since those dark days. It cures sleeping sickness with one-day treatment course of three pills and is effective for both stages of the disease, including severe cases.

Thanks to its safety profile, it can be provided at the village level. Acoziborole will therefore become a major tool to facilitate efforts to finally eliminate sleeping sickness.

🤝 This achievement reflects over a decade of collaboration and innovation across the global health community. We thank our partners, donors, staff, and everyone who was involved in making the dream of a single-dose, oral treatment for sleeping sickness come true for the people affected.

📽️ Over the next weeks, we will be sharing stories of some of  those who played a role in making medical history, and what this milestone means. Follow us to hear their voices!

👉 More information: https://bit.ly/4tYk8oj 

📢 Help share the news by using our social media toolkit: https://bit.ly/3Ojh7if

#1Dose1Cure #GlobalHealth #beatNTDs

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
It's hard to overstate how amazing this news is. When left untreated, sleeping sickness is a death sentence—but for years, the cure was nearly as dangerous as the disease itself: an arsenic-based drug that killed 1 in 20 patients.​
​
That changed in 2018 with fexinidazole, an incredible, safe, 10-day treatment. And now, sleeping sickness can be cured with a single dose of three simple pills—acoziborole—no matter how advanced it is. What once required dangerous or lengthy care can now be treated easily and safely. Thanks to Sanofi's commitment to donate the medicine, it will reach patients free of charge. I'm incredibly proud that the Gates Foundation was able to support this initiative.​

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
In many parts of sub-Saharan Africa, the biggest danger during childbirth is that a pregnant woman in crisis won’t make it to the hospital on time. ​
​
That's where m-mama comes in, using community drivers—in cars and on bikes, boats, and even horses—to get those women the transportation they desperately need. Since 2013, it has responded to more than 125,000 maternal health emergencies and saved an estimated 5,266 lives.  ​
​
When a mother or baby is in crisis, minutes can make the difference between life and death. So m-mama is making sure those minutes count.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
The NRC has approved the construction of TerraPower's advanced nuclear reactor in Kemmerer, Wyoming. This is a huge milestone—the first construction permit for a commercial-scale advanced reactor.  
 
This plant will generate 345 megawatts of carbon-free electricity, create jobs, and help move us toward a cleaner, more reliable energy future.

document.title: A Huge Win in the Fight Against Malaria

original_post.text:
Science has brought us one step closer to a world without malaria.

GanLum is the first breakthrough in malaria treatment in more than 25 years, offering hope for families across Africa. With drug resistance currently threatening decades of progress, this innovative treatment arrives at a critical moment.

It’s a bold reminder of what’s possible when we invest in science and innovation to move toward a malaria-free future.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: This anti-malarial drug is poised to become the first breakthrough in malaria treatment in almost 25 years. This incredible step forward is a testament to partnership and science—and it couldn’t have come at a more critical time. ​

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
I’m deeply sad to learn of the passing of Dr. Bill Foege. Bill was a towering figure in global health—a man who saved the lives of literally hundreds of millions of people. He was also a friend and mentor who gave me a deep grounding in the history of global health and inspired me with his conviction that the world could do more to alleviate suffering. ​
​
Bill and I often talked together about the future of global health. The legacy of his career is that many of the remarkable developments to come will have his imprint all over them. ​

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
The #Goalkeepers2030 event in Stockholm celebrated heroes working on solutions to the world’s toughest challenges. ​
​
When it comes to reducing child deaths, we can’t stop at “almost.” And Nordic leadership, innovation, and changemakers like these are making sure we don’t.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: I spend a lot of time thinking about how AI can help address fundamental challenges like poverty, hunger, and disease. One issue I keep coming back to is making great health care accessible to everyone. I believe AI can be a gamechanger in doing just that—which is why I’m so excited that the Gates Foundation is partnering with OpenAI and African leaders on an ambitious goal: supporting 1,000 healthcare clinics as they find new ways to apply AI to improve care for their communities.

original_post.text:
In line with the guidance of our Chairman HRH Prince Alwaleed bin Talal Al Saud, and reaffirming our global commitment to the eradication of polio, with the participation of our Secretary General, HRH Princess Lamia M Al Saud, and Mr. Bill Gates, Chair of the Gates Foundation, we renew a partnership spanning more than a decade and continue working together to improve the health of millions of children worldwide, supporting their access to essential healthcare and building a more sustainable future for generations to come. To learn more: https://brnw.ch/21wZixa
#TogetherForHumanity

امتدادًا لتوجيهات صاحب السمو الملكي الأمير الوليد بن طلال اَل سعود، رئيس مجلس أمنائنا، وتأكيدًا على التزامنا العالمي بالقضاء على مرض شلل الأطفال، وبمشاركة أميننا العام الأميرة لمياء بنت ماجد آل سعود، وبيل غيتس، رئيس مجلس إدارة مؤسسة غيتس، نُجدد شراكتنا الممتدة لأكثر من عشر سنوات ونواصل العمل معًا لتحسين صحة ملايين الأطفال حول العالم، حيث نستمر في دعم وصولهم إلى الرعاية الصحية الأساسية التي يستحقونها، وبناء مستقبل أكثر استدامة للأجيال القادمة
لمزيد من المعلومات
https://brnw.ch/21wZix6
#معًا_من_أجل_الإنسان

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
Thank you, HRH Alwaleed Bin Talal, for your continued leadership and commitment to polio eradication, and for your generous support through Alwaleed Philanthropies.​
​
Together with partners around the world, we are ushering in a future where no child has to live in fear of polio.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: AI tools are a powerful way to support health workers—helping them extend clinical capacity, improve quality of care, and reach more people, especially in places where resources are scarce.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
Despite cuts to aid over the past few years, I’m optimistic about the future of health. Because even though the challenges are significant, new innovations can finally help address them.​
​
In Africa, a persistent and growing shortage of health workers makes it harder for already stretched healthcare systems to reach patients who need care. Through Horizon1000, the Gates Foundation and OpenAI are supporting country-led efforts—starting in Rwanda—to explore how AI tools can help reduce the burden on clinicians, improve quality of care, and reach more patients.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text:
Of all the things humans have ever created, AI will change society the most. It will help solve many of our current problems while also bringing new challenges very different from past innovations. In agriculture, the impacts of a warming world are already visible. Rising temperatures driven by climate change are hitting farmers with the fewest resources first. AI-powered tools will soon give smallholder farmers better guidance on weather, crop disease, and soil health—helping to protect livelihoods and strengthen food security. ​
​
Healthcare will also get better with AI. Despite an incredible pipeline of medical innovation, cost and complexity continue to limit access to care around the world. Always available, high-quality medical guidance supported by AI tools will make a meaningful difference in places with too few doctors and nurses. ​
​
In education, personalized learning has long been a goal but delivering it at scale has proven difficult. AI can help teachers meet students where they are and keep more students engaged, particularly in under resourced systems. ​
​
Across climate, health, and education, the lesson is clear: Equitable outcomes will not happen on their own. To drive real progress, AI innovations must reach the people who need it most.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: While I believe the world will keep improving, it’s harder to see that today than it has been in a long time.  But it can still happen if we foster generosity, scale innovations, and manage the acceleration of artificial intelligence responsibly: https://lnkd.in/gBEyaJmq

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: I’m hopeful that the new year means new solutions—and renewed momentum—in the fight to save and improve lives around the world.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: I have always been an optimist. These days, though, my optimism comes with footnotes. For the first time this century, the world slipped backwards on key measures like childhood mortality in 2025, and if we don’t make the right decisions, this trend will continue.  At the same time, I also believe that, within the next decade, the world will not only get back on track but enter a new era of unprecedented progress. As we start 2026, I’m thinking about how the year ahead will position us for decades to come.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Friends and colleagues often ask me how I stay optimistic during hard times. Lately, that has become a difficult question to answer. The truth is, I am still an optimist. But these days, my optimism comes with footnotes. As we head into 2026, I’m thinking about how the year ahead will set us up for the decades to come: https://lnkd.in/gFUHarBX

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Losing my dad to this disease makes the fight for Alzheimer’s breakthroughs very personal to me. Recent advancements in Alzheimer’s diagnostics specifically, like blood-based tests and digital biomarkers, help me stay optimistic about the future.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: Maternal imaging is a critical way to detect issues early on and help prevent complications from pregnancy and childbirth. But typical ultrasounds are often unaffordable and inaccessible, especially for women in low-income, rural communities. That’s changing thanks to a small, handheld device that hooks up to mobile phones. It’s called a POCUS, short for point-of-care ultrasound—and it’s the kind of innovation that makes me hopeful about the future of maternal health.

original_post.text:
Five teams. One $1M prize. The future of Alzheimer's research. 

At the AlzInsights Summit 2, our semifinalists delivered powerful pitches showcasing how agentic AI can transform ADRD research—and we announced the finalists who will be competing at #ADPD2026 in Copenhagen.  

Plus, Alzheimer’s research and AI trailblazers Randall Bateman, Director of the C-BrAIn Consortium and Eric Horvitz, Chief Scientific Officer at Microsoft, shared insights on how AI is already revolutionizing brain and life sciences. 

The innovation happening in this space is extraordinary. Watch the highlights. 

Luca Foschini, Gayle Wittenberg, Ph.D., Gabrielle Strobel, Aashima Gupta, Gregory Moore MD, PhD, Tom Stocky, Hoifung Poon, Justine Levin-Allerhand, Ram Mukunda, Paola Ruiz Puentes, Marram "Beck" Olson, Duygu Tosun-Turgut, Bingxin Z., Juan Shu, Rawan Tarawneh, Jie Hou, Rupal Patel, Vijaya B. Kolachalama, Ravi Solanki, Jonathan Hoover, Adrián Noriega de la Colina, MD PhD, Arvid E. Gollwitzer, Wenrui Hao, Jeffrey R. Petrella, Yiyu Shi, Travis S. Johnson, PhD, Kuan-lin Huang, Kexin Huang, Niranjan Bose

data.text: Truly impressive work and innovation from every team.

data.poster_title: Chair, Gates Foundation and Founder, Breakthrough Energy
data.text: From health workers who travel miles to deliver care to researchers helping farmers adapt to climate change, these heroes show what commitment looks like. Their work is moving progress forward—one improved life at a time: https://b-gat.es/3L0Xx9j
